title: foundation — data retention windows
description: Foundation ships zero persistent entities → zero retention rules. The health snapshot, module registry, and import/export contract caches are transient (max 1 hour TTL). This file exists for uniform module shape and to enumerate the compliance-tier taxonomy every downstream retention.json references.

complianceTiers.public: Non-identifying, safe to expose in logs / URLs. No retention constraint.
complianceTiers.internal: Platform-internal state. Not personally identifiable. Retention driven by operational usefulness.
complianceTiers.confidential: PII, DSAR-exportable, erasable on request. Subject to GDPR Art. 5 (storage limitation) — retained no longer than necessary.
complianceTiers.restricted: Credential-adjacent secrets. Crypto-shredded on erasure. Retention driven by security policy.
complianceTiers.regulated_minor: PII of subjects under age of consent. COPPA §312.4(c) + GDPR Art. 8 — retained only while parental consent is active.
complianceTiers.regulated_health: Safeguarding / medical data. Statutory retention (e.g. UK safeguarding — 6 years post-last-contact).
complianceTiers.regulated_financial: Payment card / tax data. PCI-DSS + tax-jurisdiction retention (typically 7 years).
complianceTiers.audit_log: Immutable audit records. Retained per compliance regime + operational SLA (typically 1-7 years). Anonymised, not deleted, on erasure requests.
